title: Editing existing excel file not read & write
author: jhyunetp

comments.author: Siemienik
comments.body:
Hello @jhyunetp, I have the same issue with images (read+write to xlsx changes their possitions), I have done pull request with some fixes:
https://github.com/guyonroche/exceljs/pull/702

I also know  issue with collumn's width and row's height, i workaruond it by setting this values for each coll&row

comments.author: jhyunetp
comments.body:
> Hello @jhyunetp, I have the same issue with images (read+write to xlsx changes their possitions), I have done pull request with some fixes:
> #702
> 
> I also know issue with collumn's width and row's height, i workaruond it by setting this values for each coll&row

Hello @Siemienik , Thank you for your comment.

Those are my problems and I would like to try your solution.

 Can you tell me how to apply your solution to my package via npm??
```

"dependencies": {
    "exceljs": "github:p3rio/exceljs#build"
}
```
is this everything for me to use??


```
  "dependencies": {
    "bootstrap": "^4.1.3",
    "electron-progressbar": "^1.1.0",
    "excel4node": "^1.7.0",
    "exceljs": "github:p3rio/exceljs#build",
    "jquery": "^3.3.1",
    "jquery-ui": "^1.12.1",
    "jsonfile": "^5.0.0",
    "shell": "^0.5.0",
    "xlsx": "^0.14.1",
    "xlsx-populate": "^1.17.0"
  }
```
this is my all dependencies

npm install
npm ERR! code 128
npm ERR! Command failed: C:\cygwin64\bin\git.EXE clone --depth=1 -q -b build git://github.com/p3rio/exceljs.git C:\Users\lich_\AppData\Roaming\npm-cache\_cacache\tmp\git-clone-880bbcaa --config core.longpaths=true
npm ERR! fatal: Invalid path '/cygdrive/c/Users/lich_/AppData/Roaming/npm-cache/_cacache/tmp/git-clone-880bbcaa/C:\Users\lich_\AppData\Roaming\npm-cache\_cacache\tmp\git-clone-880bbcaa': No such file or directory
npm ERR!

npm ERR! A complete log of this run can be found in:
npm ERR!     C:\Users\lich_\AppData\Roaming\npm-cache\_logs\2018-12-06T01_11_34_330Z-debug.log

this is my error after changing my dependencies.

comments.author: Siemienik
comments.body:
@jhyunetp it looks like a problem with npm, have You installed most recent npm version?
Did You attempt to upgrade it by `npm install npm -g`?

comments.author: Siemienik
comments.body:
@jhyunetp could you write if it works for You?
Regards
